name: rabbit-housekeep
summary: Repeatable measured verify-or-flag housekeeping skill. Runs slim/cleanup against a feature, a set of features, or the whole repo in complexity-sized waves: each wave proves-it-dead-or-flags every claim, measures before/after line counts with a deterministic script, and preserves named load-bearing tokens. Reduction is REPORTED not MANDATED — an already-clean target is an honest no-op SUCCESS; the one mandatory gate is behavior preserved (the existing test suite stays green). The default DOC dimension slims doc surfaces; --code is now ADDITIVE (docs AND code, not docs OR code): it runs doc steps first then the code dimension (simplify src/ via code-simplifier + dead-src/-symbol pruning via coding-rules §6 grep-for-callers); --docs-only is the doc-only escape hatch. Also enforces the spec-rules §4 Script-Backed Orchestration standard as a script-tier verify-or-flag dimension: check-script-backed.py scans SKILL/agent/command bodies for non-script-backed orchestration steps and routes each through the prove-it-dead-or-flag disposition. Cross-feature/repo-wide scope is decomposed into per-feature sub-issues, each worked through the governed TDD path. For a user-installed run a wave still CREATES its PR for the audit trail and, unless --no-automerge is passed, AUTO-MERGES it to main on green gates via wave-automerge.py (HANDOFF gates green + PR mergeable/clean with green CI + honest reduced-or-no-op verdict); any failed gate leaves the PR open.
deprecation_criterion: when housekeeping is provided natively by the rabbit CLI as a first-class measured-reduction subcommand
